what_input_feature: A TTF/OpenType container with the 'CFF2' four-byte table tag at offset 0x0c-0x0f, a sfnt header magic '00 01 00 00' at offset 0x00-0x03, a one-entry table directory (numTables=1) and a CFF2 subtable whose header bytes ('01 00 02 0c ...' starting around offset 0x18) parse as a valid CFF2 top-dict so that hb_blob_t::as<OT::cff2>() returns a non-Null pointer at line 409.
why_winner_satisfies: All five W seeds carry the exact byte pattern '00 01 00 00 00 01 ... CFF2 ... 00 00 00 1a 01 00 02 0c' in the first 0x1c bytes, giving a sfnt header with numTables=1, a CFF2 tag, and a CFF2 top-dict that sanitizes; the W-side overlay shows execution proceeding past line 411 into the topDict parsing block at lines 414-420.
why_loser_doesnt: L seeds are scattered at the magic-byte offsets (offset 0x0c-0x0f shows 00/20/56/56 'VV', 'VA', 'GS', 'GP' table tags instead of 'CFF2'; offset 0x05 ranges across 04/20/1f/03 instead of 01); the L-side overlay shows control flow hits line 412 'goto fail' for 9/10 trials, so the cff2 pointer compared equal to &Null and the constructor aborts before topDict.init.
mechanism_attribution: Synergy of I2SRandReplace (cmplog/vpc) substituting the literal 'CFF2' four-byte tag into the input from intercepted CMPs against the table-directory iterator, AND the CMP_MAP Hamming-distance gradient (vp/vpc) preserving intermediate seeds that get progressively closer to a sanitizable CFF2 top-dict structure. cmplog alone can plant 'CFF2' but cannot incrementally satisfy the multi-byte CFF2 header validation; value_profile alone has the gradient but no literal substitution for the tag — only their union (vpc) clears both stages within the 12h budget.

what_input_feature: A PNG stream containing a syntactically valid pCAL chunk that survives CRC and length checks, where the equation type byte at pCAL_payload+9 satisfies the OR-chain's false branch — i.e. type is not PNG_EQUATION_ARBITRARY (2) and not one of the other recognized values with mismatched nparams. In the winner seeds, the type byte additionally satisfies type >= PNG_EQUATION_LAST (4), which is why the else-if at L2329 only fires in W.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog seeds reach OSS_FUZZ_png_handle_pCAL (entry-line counts diverge sharply in W vs L) and at the type byte the W cohort never matches PNG_EQUATION_ARBITRARY (DIVERGENT BRANCHES L2322 shows W T=0 F=22 for type==ARBITRARY) and proceeds into the else-if at L2329 that is hit only in W. vpc combines CMP_MAP gradient feedback (which guides toward valid chunk structure / past CRC/length gates) with I2SRandReplace substitution that can plant the small literal constants compared against type (0,1,2,3,4) directly into pCAL_payload+9, so vpc finds both the navigation and the gate-byte in one strategy.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog has I2SRandReplace but lacks CMP_MAP gradient, so it explores the upstream PNG chunk soup more sparsely and tends to land on pCAL payloads with type==PNG_EQUATION_ARBITRARY (DIVERGENT BRANCHES L2322 shows L T=11 F=0 for type==ARBITRARY combined with nparams!=3, triggering the L2325 'invalid parameter count' early-return). value_profile has CMP_MAP gradient and can navigate to the pCAL handler but without I2SRandReplace it cannot cheaply substitute the equation-type constants into the type byte, so it spends time on the same ARBITRARY+mismatched-nparams pattern and times out. naive lacks both and barely reaches the chunk (REFERENCE, 1/10).
mechanism_attribution: I2SRandReplace is the proximate technique that lands the pCAL type byte on a value satisfying the OR-chain's false side. The cmplog/vpc CmpLogObserver buffer captures the equation-type literal operands at the L2320-2323 comparisons and I2SRandReplace substitutes them into the input at the byte that flows into 'type = buf[9]'. The synergy that distinguishes vpc from cmplog (Pair 1's value_profile delta) is the value-profile distance-bucket feedback that preserves seeds making incremental progress through upstream byte-equality gates so that pCAL is reached often enough for I2SRandReplace to fire on the right offsets. Lineage of winner seed 04527fb15010de2b shows an I2S-floor (mutation_op=-) ancestor at depth 20, consistent with I2SRandReplace being on the path.

what_input_feature: Input must (a) navigate the push-parser through XML_PARSER_CONTENT to the 9-byte equality cascade '<![CDATA[' on lines 11747-11754, which sets ctxt->instate = XML_PARSER_CDATA_SECTION; AND (b) include a subsequent ']]>' 3-byte literal so that xmlParseLookupString / strstr at lines 11825-11828 returns a non-NULL pointer, taking the False branch of `if (term == NULL)`.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog produces inputs in which both '<![CDATA[' and ']]>' appear at corpus-discovery time. Synergy: only the fuzzer carrying BOTH I2SRandReplace (for substituting multi-byte literal operands like the 9-byte CDATA opener and the 3-byte ']]>') AND CMP_MAP gradient (for incrementally navigating preceding multi-byte equality cascades that gate state entry) clears the gate; the W seed's body confirms presence of these substrings.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog (loser of pair 1) has I2SRandReplace but no CMP_MAP gradient, so it cannot retain partial-prefix progress on the long byte cascades that gate entry into CDATA state — it blocks 10/10 trials. value_profile (loser of pair 2) has CMP_MAP gradient but lacks I2SRandReplace, so even when its gradient navigates into the CDATA section, it cannot synthesize the literal ']]>' terminator from CMP operands — it blocks 8/10 trials.
mechanism_attribution: Joint mechanism (synergy / 'other'): the multi-byte equality cascade gating XML_PARSER_CDATA_SECTION state plus the literal-terminator search both demand I2S-style operand substitution AND CMP_MAP-style partial-match feedback. Neither single technique suffices — only their union (value_profile_cmplog) resolves the branch.
